name: publish
description: Turns project artefacts and markdown into publication-grade output. PUBLISH mines git history, docs, and code for narrative signal and writes articles (origin stories, deep dives, retrospectives, release notes); composes standalone diagrams that respect A4-portrait legibility rules — with a Mermaid-native value-handler (the full diagram taxonomy + theming + ELK, reached through diagram-studio) alongside the Graphviz/Typst/LaTeX producers; typesets print-quality PDFs with embedded figures; runs an adversarial print/DTP + data-viz design-reviewer (typography by Bringhurst/grids, charts by Tufte/Cleveland/Bertin) in a convergent loop that genuinely improves the artefact; and surfaces a parallel prose/document-reviewer (copy-review — clarity/accuracy/tone/punchiness/tangents) invocable on any document. Use it directly on any project, or as the PUBLISHING companion the deliver plugin hands off to. Self-improving: every piece of design feedback is woven back into the shared charting matrix so the same error never recurs.

metadata.note: skills/ and commands/ are auto-discovered from the plugin root. /publish is the single front door; writer and rich-pdf-with-diagrams reference each other within this plugin via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}.
